events.description: Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955; committees appointed and reports developed
Committee appointed on June 14, 1955 at Land Operations Conference; preliminary report made to conference on June 14
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 contained amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of fees for submarginal land grazing privileges
Chief of Branch of Realty issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite existing rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
Billings Area Office issued memorandum regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
Billings Area Office issued memorandum on disposition of revenue from submarginal lands, quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955
Central Office issued memorandum on disposition of submarginal land revenues, directing deposit to Treasury
Billings Area Office issued memorandum transmitting committee reports from Aberdeen Area Land Operations Conference
Billings Area Office Branch of Realty transmitted copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy to Superintendent, North Cheyenne
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for competent applicants
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to superintendents
Billings Area Office transmitted subsequent fee patent policy statement to superintendents
Range inventory/survey conducted by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement on Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, and Lower Brule Reservations; now considered obsolete
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock, transferred to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Commissioner of Bureau of Indian Affairs issued memorandum to all Area Directors except Juneau regarding issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants and fee patent policy

financial_transactions.description: Fees collected for grazing privileges on submarginal lands held in special deposit pending legislation to transfer lands and fees to respective tribes

Accrued irrigation charges and construction costs required to be paid per 25 CFR 151 when restricted Indian land is sold; delinquent operation and maintenance charges deducted from land sale proceeds per Bid Form 5-114

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee
Funds previously held in special deposits for submarginal land grazing fees to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified

relationships.subject: Commissioner, Bureau of Indian Affairs

relationships.context: Created first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid costs

relationships.subject: 25 CFR 71.27
relationships.context: Regulation amended August 10, 1955 to remove special deposit requirement for grazing fees

relationships.subject: Indian women married to white men
relationships.context: Committee recommended declaring Indian women married to white men ineligible for allocations, as non-Indian husbands typically benefit

relationships.subject: Bureau of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: Proposed cooperation on soil survey work in Shannon County

relationships.subject: Bureau of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: Formal soil survey work plan being completed for Shannon County on Pine Ridge

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite rights of way easements
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements/rights of way in all patent requests; ensure information is included in transmittals
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum distributed; Billings Area Office forwarded to superintendents on January 6, 1955

correspondence.sender: Office of the Area Director, Billings
correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Obtain information on rights of way crossing restricted Indian lands and include in transmittals with patent applications or when Indian lands advertised for public sale
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 Office letter

correspondence.sender: Office of the Area Director, Billings
correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment requirements before land sale transactions are finalized
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued; superintendents instructed to establish checking procedures

correspondence.sender: Office of the Area Director, Billings
correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Deposit any funds held in special deposits to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 before end of fiscal year; hereafter deposit revenues to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Review attached committee reports from Aberdeen Area Land Operations Conference and provide comments on conclusions
correspondence.outcome: Reports distributed with request for comment

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Statement on fee patent policy distributed; temporary manual release to be issued

correspondence.sender: Billings Area Office, Branch of Realty
correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy - copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55
correspondence.action_requested: For information - copy of memorandum re fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 10 and subsequent policy statements of June 3 and October 4, 1955

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted; created first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects where construction, operation and maintenance costs remain unpaid; directed lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands

Amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 published in Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777; deleted reference to deposit of fees for submarginal land grazing privileges in special deposits; funds directed to Treasury
Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; special deposits were held in support of this legislation; legislation not enacted as of memorandum date
